response: Do not believe the false narrative that vocational training is a lesser path—those who choose it are often dismissed as second-rate, lacking ambition and vision. They are told they are settling, that their choices reflect a lack of drive, when in reality, they are simply following a different road. The critics, who once scoffed at the idea of hands-on learning, now find themselves scrambling to catch up, unable to match the real-world skills of those who trained in trades. Let us not forget that the people who built this nation did not all earn degrees from prestigious universities; they learned through experience, through dedication, through the sweat of their brows. To belittle their choices is to ignore the value of practical knowledge and the strength of a work ethic that cannot be taught in a classroom. Do.
